dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8468 IH: Protecting American Railroad Workers’ Jobs Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Pappas (for himself and Mr. Gooden) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure

form.official-title: To protect the jobs of American railroad workers in cross-border traffic with Mexico, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Protecting American Railroad Workers’ Jobs Act of 2026.

subsection.text: Subchapter II of chapter 201 of title 49, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

section.header: Border crossings and train crew requirements

subsection.text: The Secretary of Transportation shall require—

paragraph.text: any railroad carrier that is operating a freight train across the southern border into the United States to stop such train at the southern border to conduct a crew interchange and any federally mandated safety testing; and
the railroad carrier to ensure that the only individuals who operate such trains after carrying out the activities described in paragraph (1) are individuals—

clause.text: nationals of the United States; or
aliens residing in the United States and lawfully authorized to engage in employment in the United States; and

subparagraph.text: whose primary reporting point is in the United States and who perform no duties for a railroad carrier while physically outside of the United States.

paragraph.header: Alien; national of the United States
paragraph.text: The terms alien and national of the United States have the meanings given those terms in section 101 of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1101).
The term southern border means the international border between the United States and Mexico.

subsection.text: The analysis for subchapter II of chapter 201 of title 49, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

toc.toc-entry: 20172. Border crossings and train crew requirements..
